title: Ricinocarpus subvillosus
intro: Ricinocarpus subvillosus, also known as the "Ricinocarpus plant", is a type of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Ricinocarpus subvillosus has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ricinocarpus subvillosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Ricinocarpus subvillosus is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, teas, and tinctures to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus subvillosus is a plant species within the family Celastraceae. This classification places it in the order Celastrales. Further, it belongs to the subclass Rosidae and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Celastraceae is a group of flowering plants often known as the spindletree family. Within its classification, Ricinocarpus subvillosus shares characteristics with other plants in this family.
